channel.title: LA on Detention Pipeline
channel.description: Recent content in LA on Detention Pipeline

item.title: 287(g) JEM: Bossier Parish Sheriff’s Office (LA)
item.description:
287(g) agreement between ICE and Bossier Parish Sheriff’s Office.

Model: JEM Signed: February 28, 2025 County: Bossier Parish State: LA FIPS: 22015

item.title: Catahoula Correctional Center — Catahoula, LA
item.description:
IGSA detention facility: Catahoula Correctional Center in Catahoula County, LA.

Source: Vera Institute ICE Detention Trends (vera-institute/ice-detention-trends)

item.title: Catahoula Parish, LA — USDA Distress Profile (4 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Catahoula Parish, LA with 4 distress indicators:

losing population; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); low employment ratio; low post-secondary education

Full flags: Low Employment, Low PostSecondary Ed, Population Loss, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

Source: USDA ERS County Typology Codes, 2025 edition

item.title: Commercial Ave at I-12 — East Baton Rouge, LA (150000 SF)
item.description:
Industrial/warehouse property at Commercial Ave at I-12, Baton Rouge, East Baton Rouge County, LA. 150000 sq ft. Owner: Capitol City Produce. Status: leased.

Expanded distribution center, includes refrigerated warehouse

Source: The Advocate, 2024

item.description:

item.title: Concordia Parish, LA — USDA Distress Profile (3 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Concordia Parish, LA with 3 distress indicators:

persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); low employment ratio; low post-secondary education

Full flags: Low Employment, Low PostSecondary Ed, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

item.title: CSI AVIATION, INC — ICE RAPIDES, LA $27,713,228
item.description:
ICE contract.

Recipient: CSI AVIATION, INC Award ID: HSCECR11F00024 Amount: $27,713,227.57 Description: UNSCHEDULED AIR CHARTER SERVICES OPERATING OUT OF ALEXANDRIA, LOUISIANA. Period: 2011-05-13 to 2012-04-01 Location: RAPIDES, LA (FIPS: 22079)

item.title: Evangeline Parish, LA — USDA Distress Profile (5 indicators)
item.description:
USDA Economic Research Service county typology flags Evangeline Parish, LA with 5 distress indicators:

losing population; persistent poverty (20%+ poverty rate for 30+ years); low employment ratio; housing stress; low post-secondary education

Full flags: Housing Stress, Low Employment, Low PostSecondary Ed, Population Loss, Persistent Poverty

This county already has an IGSA detention facility. The combination of existing federal detention relationship + economic distress = high vulnerability to expansion pitch.

Nonmetro: nonmetro

item.description:

item.description:

item.title: PEOPLE WHO THINK, L.L.C. — ICE SAINT TAMMANY, LA $24,974,000
item.description:
ICE contract.

Recipient: PEOPLE WHO THINK, L.L.C. Award ID: 70CMSW25FR0000081 Amount: $24,974,000.00 Description: THIS AWARD PROCURES MEDIA CONTENT CREATION SUPPORT SERVICES FOR IMMIGRATION AND CUSTOMS ENFORCEMENT RECRUITMENT CAMPAIGN IN SUPPORT OF THE OFFICE OF PUBLIC AFFAIRS. Period: 2025-08-15 to 2026-01-01 Location: SAINT TAMMANY, LA (FIPS: )
